zh.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley是Joan fitzMartin的兒子，於1314年1月7日出生在英國諾廷加姆爾。他和Joan de Mortimer至少有1個女兒。他去世於1386年4月1日在法國享年72歲，埋葬在英國戴文艾可謝特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley是Joan fitzMartin的儿子，于1314年1月7日出生在英国諾廷加姆爾。他和Joan de Mortimer至少有1个女儿。他去世于1386年4月1日在法国享年72岁，埋葬在英国戴文艾可謝特。

nameMeanings.name: Audley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Audley in Staffordshire, named from the Old English female personal name Aldgȳth + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley was born on 7 January 1314, in Nottinghamshire, England as the son of Joan fitzMartin. He had at least 1 daughter with Joan de Mortimer. He died on 1 April 1386, in France, at the age of 72, and was buried in Exeter, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley est né le 7 janvier 1314, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, fils de Joan fitzMartin. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Joan de Mortimer. Il est décédé le 1 avril 1386, à France, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley wurde am 7. Januar 1314 in Nottinghamshire, England, als Sohn von Joan fitzMartin geboren. Er hatte mit Joan de Mortimer mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 1. April 1386 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Frankreich und wurde in Exeter, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley è nato il il 7 gennaio 1314 nel Nottinghamshire, England come figlio di Joan fitzMartin. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Joan de Mortimer. Morì il 1 aprile 1386, nel Francia, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Exeter, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audleyは1314年1月7日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでJoan fitzMartinの息子として生まれた。彼はJoan de Mortimerとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1386年4月1日にフランスで72歳で死去し，イギリスデボンエクセターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley(은)는 1314년 1월 7일 영국 노틴그함실르에서 Joan fitzMartin의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Joan de Mortimer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1386년 4월 1일 프랑스에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 세터에 묻혔다.

pt.7 January 1314–1 April 1386: 7 de janeiro de 1314–1 de abril de 1386
pt.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley nasceu em 7 de janeiro de 1314, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra filho de Joan fitzMartin. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Joan de Mortimer. Ele faleceu em 1 de abril de 1386, em França, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley родился 7 января 1314 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия. Его мать звали Joan fitzMartin. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Joan de Mortimer. Он умер 1 апреля 1386 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности эксетир, девон, Англия.

es.7 January 1314–1 April 1386: 7 de enero de 1314–1 de abril de 1386
es.lifeSummary: Sir James de Audley 2nd Baron Audley nació el 7 de enero de 1314, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Joan fitzMartin. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Joan de Mortimer. Murió el 1 de abril de 1386, en Francia, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.
